key_insight: Direct spraying of polypyrrole onto alginate nonwoven fabrics creates a uniform conductive coating that also strongly boosts solar photothermal evaporation.
engineering_relevance: This offers a simple, scalable surface-treatment route for flexible water-treatment textiles that combine conductivity, wettability control, and solar-driven desalination/dye remediation.
method: Spray-coat PPy onto alginate nonwoven fabrics (ANFs).
Characterize coating chemistry/morphology and test conductivity, wettability, photothermal behavior, and evaporation performance.
evidence_signal: At a Py dosage of 0.3 mol m−2, the PPy/ANFs reached 1.73 kg m−2 h−1 evaporation rate and 93.54% solar-thermal conversion efficiency under 1 sun.
watch_item: Performance depends on colorant type and dispersion chemistry, so real wastewater mixtures may show reduced evaporation gains versus clean-water tests.
paper_title: Spraying Polypyrrole Coatings on Alginate Nonwoven Fabrics and Investigating Their Conductive and Photothermal Properties
